note_data.title: Practical Geometry — Circles
keyPoints: Construction of tangents to a circle from an external point using compass and ruler only
A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact
From any external point, exactly two tangents can be drawn to a circle
The two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length
Construction of a circle circumscribing a triangle (circumcircle)
Construction of a circle inscribed in a triangle (incircle)
The perpendicular bisectors of the sides of a triangle meet at the circumcenter
The angle bisectors of a triangle meet at the incenter
Common tangents to two circles — external and internal tangents

definitions.definition: A tangent to a circle is a straight line which touches the circle at one point only. The point where the tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact.
A circle which passes through all the vertices of a triangle is called the circumcircle of the triangle.
A circle which touches all the sides of a triangle is called the incircle of the triangle.
The center of the circumcircle of a triangle is called the circumcenter. It is the point where the perpendicular bisectors of the sides meet.
The center of the incircle of a triangle is called the incenter. It is the point where the angle bisectors of the triangle meet.

importantQuestions.question: Define tangent to a circle. How many tangents can be drawn to a circle from an external point?
importantQuestions.answer: A tangent to a circle is a straight line which touches the circle at one point only. From any external point, exactly two tangents can be drawn to a circle, and both tangents are equal in length.

importantQuestions.question: Construct two tangents to a circle from an external point P. Write the steps of construction.
importantQuestions.answer: Steps: 1) Draw circle with center O and radius r. 2) Mark external point P. 3) Join OP and bisect it at M. 4) With center M and radius MP, draw a circle intersecting the given circle at points A and B. 5) Join PA and PB. These are the required tangents. Proof: Angle OAP = 90° (angle in semicircle), so PA ⊥ OA, making PA a tangent.

mcqs.question: The number of tangents that can be drawn to a circle from an external point is:
The circumcenter of a triangle is the intersection point of:
A tangent to a circle makes what angle with the radius at the point of contact?

commonMistakes: Students forget to prove that PA ⊥ OA in tangent construction
Confusing circumcenter with incenter — remember circumcenter uses perpendicular bisectors
Not marking the construction arcs clearly in diagrams
Forgetting that tangents from external point are equal in length
Drawing inaccurate perpendicular bisectors without proper compass technique
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 construction question (5-8 marks) in BSEK papers
Always draw neat diagrams with proper labeling — marks are given for construction steps
Memorize the exact steps of tangent construction — this appears every year
Practice using compass and ruler only — no protractor allowed in constructions
Write 'Hence proved' or 'Hence constructed' at the end of construction problems
formulas: Length of tangent from external point P to circle = √(OP² - r²)
note_data.keyDiagram: Circle with center O, external point P, tangents PA and PB touching circle at points A and B. Show construction circle with center M (midpoint of OP) passing through A and B. Mark all angles and equal lengths clearly.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (4 marks) — asked students to construct a tangent to a circle from an external point and justify the construction steps
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q6 (5 marks) — required definition of tangent, secant, and chord with labeled diagram showing all three
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q3 (3 marks) — asked to prove that tangent at any point of circle is perpendicular to radius through point of contact
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q5 (4 marks) — construction of circle passing through three non-collinear points with compass and ruler only

workedExample.question: Define tangent to a circle. Construct a tangent to a given circle from an external point P. (5 marks)
steps: Definition: A tangent to a circle is a straight line that touches the circle at exactly one point
Draw circle with center O and mark external point P
Join OP and bisect it at point M
With M as center and MO as radius, draw a circle intersecting given circle at points A and B
Join PA and PB. These are the required tangents from P to the circle
workedExample.answer: A tangent to a circle is a straight line that touches the circle at exactly one point. Construction completed using compass and ruler showing both tangents PA and PB from external point P.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct definition, 1 mark for drawing circle and marking point P, 1 mark for bisecting OP, 1 mark for auxiliary circle construction, 1 mark for drawing tangents PA and PB

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing 'tangent touches the circle' instead of the complete textbook definition 'tangent is a straight line that touches the circle at exactly one point'
Many students confuse secant and chord definitions - remember secant extends beyond the circle while chord has both endpoints on the circle
In construction questions, always show compass marks and construction lines clearly, as marks are awarded for proper geometric construction steps, not just the final diagram
note_data.quickRecap: Practical geometry of circles involves precise definitions and constructions. Tangent touches circle at one point, secant intersects at two points, chord connects two points on circle. Key constructions include tangents from external points using auxiliary circles, and circles through three points using perpendicular bisectors. Always use compass and ruler for constructions.
importantFormulas: Power of point: PA × PB = PC × PD (for secants from external point)
Tangent length from external point: PT² = PO² - r² (where O is center, r is radius)
Tangent ⊥ radius at point of contact
